text: H-EXT (reasoning externalization): tool granularity does not reduce solving work, it relocates it. The no-tools agent solves in very few (3-4) but token-heavy turns; tool agents take many (25-122) token-light turns, one deduction per round-trip. So granularity multiplies turns 8-23x while cutting generated output tokens up to ~2.8x, and tool calls themselves are nearly free (confirms a-0012). The dollar gap none-coarse is Goldilocks-shaped (peaks ~0.55x at the mid rung, ~0 at easy and hard): at easy the no-tools baseline is already cheap; at hard the many tool turns each re-read a growing transcript, eroding coarse's per-turn advantage. This is the mechanism behind a-0013.
